ctxMarket: Attention is focused on Drift Protocol’s major recovery effort after its recent exploit, with Tether stepping in to provide significant backing. There is also interest in new trading infrastructure and political activity linked to Solana.
ctxTalking:
• Drift Protocol is relaunching with up to $148 million in support from Tether, moving away from Circle’s USDC after a large exploit.
• DoubleZero is introducing high-speed, real-time Solana data feeds for traders.
• A Solana Institute-backed super PAC is spending $8 million in the Ohio Senate race.
ctxMatters: Drift’s pivot to USDT and Tether’s support highlight shifting stablecoin preferences and risk management after DeFi exploits. Faster blockchain data from DoubleZero could impact trading strategies and liquidity on Solana. Political spending by Solana-linked groups signals growing crypto influence in U.S. elections.
ctxSignal:
Signal: Tether’s direct role in DeFi recovery and the move away from USDC; new trading tech for Solana.
Noise: Speculation about Circle’s response to the exploit without clear outcomes.
ctxGlossary:
Perpetuals DEX — a decentralized exchange for perpetual futures contracts.
Super PAC — a political action committee that can raise and spend unlimited funds to influence elections.

readMore.title: Drift Taps Tether for $148 Million Recovery Plan, Ditches Circle's USDC Following DeFi Exploit
Tether backs Drift recovery with $127.5M as platform shifts from USDC to USDT
Tether announces $150M recovery program for Drift Protocol
